chapter_question: What data must a map carry so lengths, angles, and curvature can be recovered?
inspection_targets: Tissot ellipses show how coordinate steps become measured lengths.
Projection anisotropy appears as local ellipses.
The coefficients A, B, and F measure a coordinate parallelogram.
The same scale in every direction preserves angles.
Circles and lines survive the projection as inspectable images.

source_span.focus: Develops the metric as the intrinsic data preserved or distorted by maps, with stereographic projection as the main example.
source_span.label: Chapter 04
source_span.title: Mapping Surfaces: The Metric
topics: Metric curvature formula as a preview of intrinsic computation
Circle preservation and explicit stereographic formulas
